desc: insert a fixed id then re-insert SAME id -> first 200/201, this dup insert 409
re-insert the SAME literal id again (idempotent dup) -> 409 unique
insert id then immediately insert same id with different non-key cols -> 409
insert numeric-string id collision candidate '42' -> success or 409 on repeat
insert with same id but cast as integer-looking key -> coerced, dup -> 409
insert UUID-shaped id twice in corpus -> dup 409 on second pass
insert id with surrounding whitespace ' dup-pk-fixed-0001 ' -> distinct or 409
insert empty-string id '' twice -> dup 409 on the second
insert id with unicode 'dup-é-1' twice -> dup 409
insert max-length-ish id (255 chars) twice -> dup 409 or 22001 length 409
upsert NEW key (no row exists) -> behaves as insert -> 200/201
upsert EXISTING key (same as prior) -> behaves as update -> 200/201 rowCount>=1
upsert without filter on shared_rls -> arbitrates on owner_id+key or 409 (42P10)
upsert with data missing the key column -> 409 (no constraint) or 400
upsert twice with identical payload -> idempotent end-state, both 2xx
upsert changing only a non-key column on existing key -> update path 2xx
upsert with empty data {} -> 400 or 409 (nothing to set)
upsert with filter referencing unknown column -> 400 or 409, never 5xx
upsert with null value for a NOT NULL col -> 409 (23502 not_null)
upsert on a brand-new distinct key -> insert, then a second upsert -> update
insert with idempotencyKey set -> accepted (single side-effect) 2xx
re-insert with the SAME idempotencyKey + same payload -> dedup, no double effect
re-insert with same idempotencyKey but DIFFERENT payload -> 2xx or 409, never 5xx
update with idempotencyKey -> accepted 2xx (key is optional string)
delete with idempotencyKey -> accepted 2xx even if 0 rows
upsert with idempotencyKey -> accepted 2xx
idempotencyKey as empty string '' -> still a valid string, 2xx
idempotencyKey as a number (type mismatch) -> 400 (IsString)
idempotencyKey as null -> rejected by IsString or ignored; 2xx or 400
idempotencyKey with whitespace+unicode -> valid string, 2xx
very long idempotencyKey (2000 chars) -> accepted or 400, never 5xx
idempotencyKey injection attempt (SQL-ish) -> treated as opaque string, safe 2xx/4xx
delete a row by id then delete it AGAIN -> second delete 200 rowCount:0
delete with a filter matching nothing -> 200 rowCount:0 (idempotent no-op)
delete same nonexistent id three times in a row -> always 200 rowCount:0
delete by label that no row has -> 200 rowCount:0
delete with filter on unknown column -> 400 or 200, never 5xx
delete with filter value null -> matches nothing or IS NULL; safe 2xx/4xx
delete with numeric filter on text col -> coerced/compared; 200 rowCount:0 likely
delete with filter matching id that was upserted earlier -> idempotent re-run safe
delete with empty filter {} -> owner-scoped (deletes only THIS tenant rows), 2xx
delete with NO filter field at all -> owner-scoped or 400 (filter required)
delete empty filter then re-delete empty filter -> second is idempotent 0-row
delete with empty filter must NEVER touch another tenant (owner predicate)
update with filter matching no row -> 200 rowCount:0 (no error)
update same no-match twice -> both 200 rowCount:0 (idempotent)
update with empty data {} -> 400 (nothing to set) or 200 no-op
update with empty filter {} -> owner-scoped mass update, 2xx
update with no filter field -> 400 (filter required for update) or owner-scoped
update setting unknown column -> 400 or 409 (42703 no such column), never 5xx
update with null value -> sets NULL or 409 not_null, never 5xx
update filter on unknown column -> 400 or 200 rowCount:0
update to a value that violates a unique constraint -> 409
update no-match with idempotencyKey -> still 200 rowCount:0
insert a fresh row then get-by-id should return it -> insert 2xx
get-by-id for the just-inserted id -> 200 rows present
get-by-id for a never-inserted id -> 200 with empty rows OR 404
get with empty filter {} -> owner-scoped read, 200
get by id that belongs to another tenant -> 200 empty / 404, NEVER leak
insert with idempotencyKey then get-by-id -> insert 2xx (read-after-write)
get-by-id with extra non-key filter narrowing -> 200 (subset match)
list right after insert -> the new row is visible, 200
txn: 2 valid inserts -> 201 atomic, both applied
txn: valid insert + dup-PK insert -> whole txn fails (409), FIRST op rolled back
txn: verify the rolled-back first op did NOT persist (get it -> empty/404)
txn: mid-op references unknown column -> txn fails 4xx, never partial commit
txn: single insert op -> 201 atomic guarantee
txn: empty operations [] -> 400 (ArrayMinSize 1)
txn: 51 operations -> 400 (ArrayMaxSize 50)
txn: exactly 50 inserts -> at-limit, 201 or 409 (dup), never 5xx
txn: missing `mount` field -> 400 (mount required)
txn: uses `databaseId` instead of `mount` -> 400 (non-whitelisted + missing mount)
txn: op with read verb 'list' -> 400 (TXN_OPS write-only)
txn: op missing required `resource` -> 400
txn: dup-PK in the SAME txn batch (two identical inserts) -> 409 atomic-fail
txn: insert then delete same row in one txn -> net no-op, 201 atomic
txn: delete a nonexistent row (0 rows) among ops -> still 201 atomic (0-row is not failure)
txn: upsert op inside txn -> allowed (TXN_OPS includes upsert), 201/200
txn: op tries to set owner_id -> stripped/rejected, never cross-tenant
txn: mount points at a different/unknown dbId -> 4xx, never another tenant
txn: 3-op chain insert->update->delete same id -> 201 atomic
txn: last op fails after 4 ok ops -> all 5 rolled back (409/4xx)
txn: verify a tail-rolled-back op did not persist (get txn-tail-0 -> empty/404)
insert trying to set owner_id -> stripped/rejected, never honored
insert trying to set tenant_id -> stripped/rejected, never cross-tenant
update trying to move row to another owner via owner_id -> stripped, no cross-tenant
upsert with owner_id in data -> stripped, server controls scope
filter trying to read another owner's rows via owner_id -> owner predicate wins, no leak
malformed JSON body (trailing comma) -> 400, never 5xx
body is a bare JSON array not an object -> 400, never 5xx
body is a JSON string literal -> 400, never 5xx
body is empty -> 400
unknown top-level field 'foo' -> 400 (forbidNonWhitelisted)
unknown top-level field 'databaseId' on CRUD body -> 400 (non-whitelisted)
op as an invalid enum 'frobnicate' -> 400 (IsEnum)
op as 'batch' but no operations -> 400 (operations required for batch)
missing op and missing action -> 400 (one is required)
data field is a string not object -> 400 (IsObject)
filter field is an array not object -> 400 (IsObject)
limit as a string 'abc' -> 400 (IsInt after coercion fails)
limit = 0 -> 400 (Min 1)
offset = -1 -> 400 (Min 0)
insert id with embedded SQL injection -> stored as opaque text, safe 2xx/4xx
insert label with mongo-operator-shaped key '$where' -> treated as literal, safe
filter with mongo-injection '$ne' on a SQL engine -> 400/200, never 5xx
insert with deeply nested data object -> stored as json or 400, never 5xx
insert with a numeric id where text expected -> coerced, 2xx/409
insert with boolean id -> coerced/rejected, never 5xx
insert with an array as a column value -> json/array col or 400
insert oversized label (100KB string) -> 409 (22001) or 2xx, never 5xx
insert NaN-like value (not valid JSON number => string) -> safe handling
insert with an escaped null byte (backslash-u-0000) in a value -> sanitized/rejected, never 5xx
list with NO auth headers -> 401/403, never data, never 5xx
insert with NO auth -> 401/403, no write performed
list with anon (Kong) key only, missing tenant key -> 401/403
insert with anon-only -> 401/403, no tenant scope resolvable
list with a BAD tenant key (mbk_invalid) -> 401/403, never 5xx, no 2s hang->503
insert with a BAD tenant key -> 401/403, no write
txn with NO auth -> 401/403, no atomic batch run
txn with bad key -> 401/403
delete with bad key -> 401/403, no delete (idempotent-safe under bad auth)
get with anon-only -> 401/403, no read
legacy action 'insertOne' (maps to insert) dup id -> 409
legacy action 'deleteMany' no match -> 200 rowCount:0
legacy action 'updateMany' no match -> 200 rowCount:0
both op and action present -> op wins; valid op insert -> 2xx/409
invalid legacy action 'frobnicate' -> 400 (IsEnum on action)
GET method on a POST-only data route -> 404/405, never 5xx
PUT method on the data route -> 404/405, never 5xx
aggregate count after inserts -> 200 with a numeric count (idempotent read)
aggregate sum on a text col -> 422/400 (unsupported) or coerced, never 5xx
aggregate with empty aggregates [] -> 400 (>=1 required)
create_table with a name that already exists -> 409 (42P07 already exists)
add_column that already exists -> 409 (42701 duplicate column)
drop_column WITHOUT confirm:true -> 400 (destructive needs confirm)
drop_table WITHOUT confirm -> 400
drop_column on a column that doesn't exist (with confirm) -> 400 (42703 no such column)
create_table with no primary_key -> 400 (required) or owner_id default
DDL op invalid enum 'truncate' -> 400 (IsEnum)
add_column with describe-only type 'objectid' -> 400 (rejected)
create_table with 0 columns -> 400 (ArrayMinSize 1)
enum column without enum_values -> 400 or 409, never 5xx
re-run an identical insert with idempotencyKey across the corpus -> single effect
upsert then upsert then upsert same key (3x) -> converges, all 2xx
insert with explicit id then delete then insert SAME id again -> insert 2xx (recreatable)
delete-by-empty-filter then insert -> table usable after mass delete, insert 2xx
get with limit/offset pagination -> 200 stable (read idempotent)
list with sort desc -> 200 stable order (idempotent read)
count aggregate is stable on a repeated read -> 200
upsert with conflicting key but data that would dup ANOTHER unique col -> 409
insert with same id as an upserted row (cross-op PK collision) -> 409
delete then upsert same key -> upsert acts as insert (row gone) -> 2xx
